page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The underlying virtualization is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks place this instance primarily in the lower tiers for modern workloads. Stress-ng and Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests categorize its raw compute performance in the bottom 25%. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks fall into the weak category. However, the processor demonstrates average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside average text processing capabilities. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 10%.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and older Intel Xeon architecture, the ecs.c4.xlarge offers an entry-level compute-focused profile. It is qualitatively cost-effective for legacy workloads that require dedicated CPU execution without the overhead of high-capacity memory. Due to its weak database, Redis, and static web serving benchmark results, it is best suited for lightweight, non-critical compute tasks, basic single-threaded processing, or development and testing environments rather than high-throughput production databases or intensive web serving.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c4.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2667 v4 processor running at 3.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with KVM virtualization, it is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark evaluations indicate generally weak performance, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and memory bandwidth tests scoring in the bottom 25%. However, it achieves average results in single-threaded gzip compression and text processing. Given these performance characteristics and its dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, basic single-threaded processing, and development or testing environments where high memory capacity is not required.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring four Intel Xeon cores and eight gigabytes of memory for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
